name: Amritsar
summary: Amritsar is generally safe for tourists visiting the Golden Temple and major attractions, with violent crime being rare. However, visitors should be cautious of petty crime, transportation scams, and harassment, particularly in crowded areas and markets.
quickVerdict: Amritsar offers a relatively safe travel experience for most visitors, with the city's strong Sikh community known for hospitality and the major tourist areas well-policed. While violent crime against tourists is uncommon, travelers should remain alert for pickpocketing, auto-rickshaw overcharging, and tourist-targeted scams. The city's spiritual atmosphere around the Golden Temple provides a secure environment, but standard precautions are advised when exploring markets and traveling at night.

neighborhoods.name: Golden Temple Complex & Surrounding Area
neighborhoods.description: The most secure area in Amritsar with heavy security presence and volunteer protection. The Sikh community maintains excellent safety standards around this sacred site.

neighborhoods.name: Ranjit Avenue
neighborhoods.description: Modern commercial district with wide roads, upscale hotels, restaurants and shopping centers. Well-lit and generally safe with good police presence.

neighborhoods.name: Lawrence Road
neighborhoods.description: Commercial area with shops, cafes and eateries. Busy during day with decent safety, though caution needed at night.

neighborhoods.name: Hall Bazaar & Old City
neighborhoods.description: Traditional market area with narrow crowded streets. Higher risk of pickpocketing and petty crime, especially for solo travelers.

neighborhoods.name: Railway Station Area
neighborhoods.description: Busy transport hub with reports of harassment and scams. Exercise extra caution, especially at night and when carrying luggage.

neighborhoods.name: Outskirts & Rural Areas
neighborhoods.description: Less developed areas with limited police presence and street lighting. Avoid traveling alone, particularly after dark.

scams.name: Auto-Rickshaw Overcharging
scams.description: Drivers refuse to use meters and quote inflated fares, especially to tourists. Some claim hotels are closed to take you to commission-paying accommodations.
scams.howToAvoid: Always negotiate fare beforehand or insist on meter. Use ride-hailing apps like Uber or Ola for transparent pricing. Ask your hotel about fair rates.

scams.name: Fake Government Tourist Offices
scams.description: Fraudulent offices near tourist areas offer overpriced packages and fake tickets, claiming to be official government services.
scams.howToAvoid: Only book through verified official tourism websites. Check reviews online and verify credentials before paying any money.

scams.name: Airport Parking Overcharging
scams.description: Parking contractors at Amritsar airport deliberately delay vehicles in free zones to force payment and switch off digital payment systems to collect cash.
scams.howToAvoid: Keep track of time in free zones, insist on digital payments, and report overcharging to airport authorities immediately.

scams.name: Shopping & Tailoring Scams
scams.description: Shops promise high-quality tailoring with next-day delivery but deliver poor quality products and refuse refunds after payment.
scams.howToAvoid: Research shops beforehand, read reviews, avoid paying full amount upfront, and inspect quality before accepting items.

soloFemale.overview: Solo female travel in Amritsar is possible but requires extra caution. The local Sikh community is generally hospitable, but reports of unwanted attention and harassment exist, particularly in crowded areas and markets. Women-only transport compartments are available in some areas.
tips: Dress conservatively covering legs and shoulders, especially around religious sites
Use women-only transport compartments when available
Avoid traveling alone at night and stay in well-lit populated areas
Book accommodation with good security and reviews from other female travelers
Keep emergency contacts handy and inform someone of your whereabouts

nightSafety.overview: Amritsar's nightlife is relatively limited and centers around hotel bars and restaurants rather than late-night street activities. Most venues close by 1 AM, and the Golden Temple area remains well-lit and patrolled. However, caution is advised in other areas after dark.
tips: Stick to well-lit main roads and avoid isolated areas after 10 PM
Use reputable transportation services or pre-booked taxis for night travel
Stay in groups when possible and avoid walking alone after dark
Keep valuables secure and carry minimal cash
Inform hotel staff of your evening plans and expected return time

transport.metro: No metro system exists in Amritsar. The city has a MetroBus system operating on a 31km route with 93 buses, but traffic congestion is common.
transport.rideshare: Uber and Ola operate in Amritsar with good safety features including emergency assistance buttons, driver profiles, and transparent pricing. Recommended over traditional taxis.
transport.taxis: Traditional taxis and auto-rickshaws often overcharge tourists and may not use meters. Airport taxis are safer with fixed pricing when pre-booked through hotels or official counters.
transport.tips: Use ride-hailing apps when possible, negotiate fares beforehand if using local transport, and avoid unofficial taxis at the airport or railway station.

customs: Cover your head when entering the Golden Temple or any gurdwara - scarves are provided free at entrances
Remove shoes and wash hands before entering the Golden Temple complex
Dress modestly with covered shoulders and legs, especially at religious sites
Use 'Namaste' greeting with palms together and slight bow when meeting locals
Avoid pointing feet toward people or religious images, and don't touch someone's head
Eat with your right hand only as the left hand is considered unclean
Show respect to elders and religious figures by standing or sitting lower than them

health.overview: Amritsar has decent healthcare facilities with several government and private hospitals. The Government Medical College and Hospital, established in 1864, and Guru Nanak Dev Hospital provide comprehensive medical services. Air quality can be poor during winter months.
health.water: Avoid tap water and drink only bottled or properly purified water. Be cautious with ice in drinks and street food that may be washed with tap water.
health.vaccinations: Routine vaccinations should be up to date. Consider Hepatitis A and B, Typhoid, and Japanese Encephalitis. Consult a travel medicine specialist before departure.
health.altitude: Amritsar is at low altitude (230m). Main health concerns are heat during summer months (May-July) and poor air quality during winter. Monsoon season brings humidity and potential waterborne diseases.

emergency.usEmbassy: US Embassy New Delhi: +91-11-2419-8000. US Consulate General Mumbai: +91-22-2672-4000. For emergencies involving US citizens: +91-11-2419-8000

faq.a: Yes, Amritsar is generally safe for tourists, especially around the Golden Temple area which has strong security. The city has low violent crime rates, though visitors should be cautious of petty crime and scams.
Amritsar is moderately safe at night in main areas like Ranjit Avenue and near the Golden Temple. However, avoid isolated areas after 10 PM and use reputable transportation services for night travel.

faq.q: Is Amritsar safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Solo female travel is possible but requires extra caution. Dress conservatively, avoid isolated areas at night, use women-only transport when available, and stay in well-reviewed accommodations with good security.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Amritsar?
faq.a: Exercise caution in crowded markets like Hall Bazaar, the railway station area especially at night, and avoid rural outskirts after dark. The old city areas can have higher pickpocketing risks.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Amritsar?
faq.a: Public buses are generally safe but can be crowded. Ride-hailing apps like Uber and Ola are safer than traditional auto-rickshaws which may overcharge. Always negotiate fares beforehand if using local transport.
